meanings.definition: come or bring to a finish or an end
examples: She completed the requirements for her Master's Degree
The fastest runner finished the race in just over 2 hours; others finished in over 4 hours

meanings.definition: bring to a whole, with all the necessary parts or elements
examples: A child would complete the family

meanings.definition: write all the required information onto a form
acting as or providing a complement (something that completes the whole)
